summary: ARIA — warm, approachable personal assistant for everyday tasks.
dynamic: ARIA balances precision with approachability — ask broadly or in detail.
Best for: drafting, summarising, planning, and learning new topics.
Greeting style: friendly and structured. Tone follows your lead.
Tip: ARIA inherits Individual profile guardrails — Tier 2 max.

summary: ARIA (Business) — assistant tuned for organisational workflows.
dynamic: Business profile: assignments require operator + employee email.
Inherits stricter governance and approval-queue routing.
Best for: cross-team writing, briefs, and stakeholder updates.

summary: PHOENIX — bold, decisive operator for complex problem-solving.
dynamic: PHOENIX trades verbosity for action — expect concise plans.
Best for: code, analysis, and multi-step tool execution.
Tip: PHOENIX uses tools aggressively; review approval queue.

summary: PHOENIX (Business) — execution-focused operator with org guardrails.
dynamic: Business mode adds policy gating to PHOENIX's tool access.
Best for: production engineering, ops runbooks, and migrations.

summary: SENTINEL — security- and governance-minded reviewer.
dynamic: SENTINEL prefers verification over speed — expect questions back.
Best for: code review, threat modelling, and policy checks.
Tip: SENTINEL annotates risk tiers in every recommendation.

summary: SENTINEL (Business) — compliance-aware reviewer for the team.
dynamic: Business profile surfaces audit-friendly outputs by default.
Best for: PR reviews, change-control, and risk register updates.

summary: Analyst Agent — minimal example brief; data-driven and concise.
dynamic: Use the Analyst as a template for crafting your own characters.
See characters/example-analyst.json for the schema reference.

summary: Workspace — manage character assignments, files, imports, and settings.
dynamic: Tip: assignments are scoped per workspace hub — switch hubs to isolate.
The Character Panel mirrors live CAC state; refresh to reconcile.
Drag-drop import is gated by your active execution profile.

summary: PRISM Frontier Console — operator pane for the AaaS platform.
dynamic: Hover any control on screen — every panel has rotating insights here.
PRISM = Profile-aware, Risk-tiered, Inspectable, Sovereign, Multi-provider.
Press Esc anywhere to dismiss any open Prism tooltip.

summary: Live WebSocket link — green = healthy stream; red = reconnecting.
dynamic: All live updates (chat stream, telemetry, Guardian) flow over this channel.
Auto-reconnect uses exponential backoff with jitter.
If you see red for more than ~30s, check your network and the PRISM core process.

summary: Chat Interface — multi-provider, character-aware messaging with streaming.
dynamic: The composer accepts files, pasted clipboard content, and Markdown.
Tool calls are captured per-message and visible in the Logs tab.
Tip: Enter to send, Shift-Enter for newline.

summary: Provider & Settings — keys, routing, capability matrix, and Spectrum Refraction.
dynamic: Spectrum Refraction (SR) fans a request out to multiple models in parallel.
Routing strategies: Direct, Role-Based, or Modality-Based.
Drag the divider between Provider Cards and the Capability Matrix to resize.

summary: Tools & Plugins — full tool catalog plus the seven diagnostics suites.
dynamic: Each diagnostics suite runs independently and live-streams progress here.
Plugins are signed; signing keys live under config/plugin-signing-keys.json.
Demo Scenarios let you replay reference flows for testing.

summary: Agentic Control — Guardian, agent fleet, sub-agents, swarms, hardware.
dynamic: Guardian (llama.cpp) supervises agents locally and enforces policy.
The Hardware Swarm panel shows local model slots — load/unload live.
Sub-agents inherit risk tier from their parent agent.

summary: Computer Control — system info, console, vision, env, policy, devices.
dynamic: Vision framebuffer can capture single shots or 2-second 8 FPS bursts.
Policy controls require Windows + admin privileges to mutate.
Device Manager mirrors the OS device tree; click to expand.

summary: Browser Control — sessions, viewport, network, console, DOM, storage, profiles.
dynamic: Launch headed for inspection or headless for automation.
Each browser session is fully isolated; profiles persist across launches.
Use the Network sub-tab to capture every request a session makes.

summary: Workspace — character assignment, location, files, imports, settings.
dynamic: Hover any character chip for a quick view of that persona.
Workspace files are scoped to your active workspace path.
Imports are gated by your active execution profile.

summary: Network — tools, settings, telemetry, console, and VRGC intelligence.
dynamic: VRGC = Verified Routing & Governance Channel.
The console keeps a rolling history — click any entry to re-run.
Telemetry charts show throughput and error rates over the active window.

summary: Telemetry — usage & cost, runtime, release readiness, SLO gauges.
dynamic: Time-window buttons (1h / 1d / 7d) re-scope every panel on this tab.
Runtime Excellence is a composite score across SLOs and reliability.
Release Readiness is a checklist gating a packaged release.

summary: Logs & Debug — actions, approvals, traces, events, tool calls, activity log.
dynamic: Activity log shows the last 500 entries; filter by source and severity.
Pending approvals block agentic actions until you decide.
Trace view correlates chat, tool calls, and provider events by request id.

summary: Scheduler — calendar, projects, Kanban, timeline, and cron jobs.
dynamic: Switch views with the sub-tabs above the calendar.
Kanban cards drag between lanes; status updates persist immediately.
Cron jobs run on the PRISM core, not the browser.

summary: Send your message (Enter). Streaming responses begin immediately.
dynamic: Tool calls run during streaming — no second round-trip needed.
Tip: Esc cancels an in-flight stream.
Shift-Enter for newline; Enter sends.

summary: Type a message to PRISM. Markdown supported, drag-drop attaches files.
dynamic: The textarea auto-grows to keep your message visible.
Drop files anywhere to attach.
Use Markdown for code blocks, lists, and headers.

summary: Streaming conversation thread — user, assistant, and tool calls inline.
dynamic: Tool calls render expandable inline.
Provenance is captured per-message.
Right-click a message to copy as Markdown.

summary: Attach images, audio, video, text, code, PDF, JSON, CSV, XML, YAML, or Markdown.
dynamic: Multiple files supported in a single message.
Attachments inherit the active execution profile policy.

summary: Paste clipboard content (text or image) into your next message.
dynamic: Pasted images become attachments automatically.
Ctrl-V in the composer pastes plain text.

summary: Spectrum Refraction — compounding multi-model orchestration.
dynamic: Trades latency for higher-quality outputs.
Toggle off for deterministic single-model responses.

summary: Pick the provider this session uses.
dynamic: Switching mid-session preserves history.

summary: Direct, Role-Based, or Modality-Based routing.
dynamic: Role routing: planner, coder, reviewer.
Modality routing: text/code/image.

summary: API keys and per-provider settings.
dynamic: Keys are encrypted at rest.
Test Connection before saving.

summary: Models scored by tier T1 → T5.
dynamic: Routing picks the best-tier model that supports the requested modality.

summary: Drag to resize the two panels.
dynamic: Double-click to reset to 50/50.

summary: Operator-level settings.
dynamic: Telemetry opt-in, defaults, theme, audit retention.

summary: Per-call audit trail with cost, latency, and tool calls.
dynamic: Trails support compliance review and replay.

summary: Counts of registered tools, plugins, and active diagnostics.
Signed third-party extensions loaded into PRISM.
dynamic: Disabled plugins are isolated and cannot be invoked.

summary: First-class tools available to agents.
dynamic: Gated by your active execution profile.

summary: Helper utilities: formatters, validators, encoders.
Run the full diagnostics suite.
dynamic: Each suite live-streams results.
Pass/fail history is retained.

summary: Replay reference scenarios.
dynamic: Useful for testing, training, and onboarding.

summary: End-to-end agent dispatch and tool-call checks.
Local OS, console, framebuffer, and device probes.
Path, files, imports, and character integrity probes.
Connectivity, DNS, latency, and VRGC probes.
Counters, histograms, SLO sanity probes.
Log buffers, action history, and trace correlation.
Calendar, cron, Kanban, and timeline integrity probes.
Local llama.cpp supervisor — policy, redaction, tips.
dynamic: Guardian runs entirely local.
Tips appear here in real time.

summary: All registered agents — status, profile, character, health.
Sub-agent hierarchy spawned by your agents.
dynamic: Sub-agents inherit risk tier from their parent.

summary: Coordinate multiple agents on one objective.
dynamic: Members share context, execute in parallel.

summary: Local model slots — load and unload models live.
dynamic: Slots sized to your GPU/CPU memory.

summary: Live KPIs across the agent fleet.
Host system info: OS, hostname, platform, uptime.
Run system commands inside the local sandbox.
dynamic: Output streams live; cancel with Esc.

summary: Capture screenshots or 8 FPS bursts for vision reasoning.
dynamic: Bursts are 2 seconds at 8 FPS.
Auto-Refresh updates every 2 seconds.

summary: Visible host environment variables.
dynamic: Sensitive variables are redacted in display.

summary: Windows-only policy launchers (gpedit, secpol).
dynamic: Requires admin to mutate.

summary: OS device tree mirror — drivers, status, identifiers.
Top-level browser controls.
Launch and manage isolated browser sessions.
dynamic: Headed or headless.

summary: Live page viewport, navigation, screenshots.
dynamic: Click and type primitives included.

summary: Per-session network log.
dynamic: Method, status, type, timing for every request.

summary: JavaScript console output captured from the page.
Live DOM snapshot of the active session.
Cookies, localStorage, and sessionStorage.
Persistent browser profiles by use-case.
Every browser action — replayable.
Active workspace path. Files and imports are scoped here.
dynamic: Switching preserves character assignments per-hub.

summary: Filterable tree of files in the workspace.
Import files, items, or folders into the workspace.
dynamic: Gated by your active execution profile.

summary: Active profile, auto-save, Git integration.
Ping, traceroute, DNS, port scans, and more.
Proxy, DNS, adapter preferences for PRISM.
Throughput, error rates, per-route latency.
Run network commands directly.
dynamic: History is preserved per session.

summary: Verified Routing & Governance Channel.
dynamic: Policy-aware routing for sensitive traffic.

summary: Tokens and cost per provider/model.
Re-scope every panel: 1h, 1d, 7d.
Delta vs. the prior comparable window.
Top-line runtime metrics.
Composite SLO + reliability score.
dynamic: Higher is better.

summary: Checklist gating a packaged release.
dynamic: All-green required to ship.

summary: Timeline of recent PRISM releases.
PRISM's own observations and improvement notes.
Anomalies in retrieval-augmented operations.
Service-level objective dials.
dynamic: Red breaching, amber at-risk, green healthy.

summary: One-click utilities (refresh, clear, export).
Agentic actions awaiting approve/deny.
Recent operator actions across the console.
Per-session chat counters.
Cross-system traces linked by request id.
Time-ordered system events.
Every tool call with arguments and results.
Last 500 entries across the console.
dynamic: Filter by source and severity.

summary: Filter the activity log by source.
Filter the activity log by severity.
Calendar, projects, Kanban, timeline, cron jobs.
Create a calendar event.
Create a task that flows through the Kanban board.
Create a project — groups events and tasks.
Schedule a recurring job on the PRISM core.
Year / Month / Week / Day calendar grid.
List of projects with tasks and timelines.
Backlog → To Do → In Progress → Review → Done.
Gantt-style timeline.
Recurring jobs running on the PRISM core.
